data.title: Aspen Aerogels sees Q3 revenue of $117M
data.description:
• Aspen Aerogels (NYSE:ASPN) on Wednesday said it expects Q3 revenue of around $117M.
  • Consensus for Q3 revenue is $95.07M.
  • ASPN +27.31% premarket to $28.57.
  • The company also said it expects quarterly net loss of ~$13M and quarterly adjusted EBITDA of about $25M.
  • Cash and cash equivalents were ~$113M as of September 30, 2024.
  • The company announced that it has received a conditional commitment from the U.S. Department of Energy for a proposed loan of up to $670.6M for financing the construction of its second aerogel manufacturing facility in Georgia.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow are muted after Wall Street closes lower on tech sell off
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages trade cautiously, a day after chip stocks slumped on a disappointing outlook from ASML (ASML).

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was near even, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was unchanged.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.01%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 2 basis points to 3.92%.

Wall Street closed in the red on Tuesday, as chip stocks took it on the chin following disappointing earnings from Dutch giant ASML (ASML) and news that the U.S. was mulling further export caps.

"Some rare weakness has come through in risk over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 falling back from its record high the previous day," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

ASML (ASML) shares were down 4% in premarket trade, after the firm said it expects 2025 net sales forecast to be between €30B to €35B (mid-point of €32.5B). The Dutch company also missed its third-quarter bookings estimates.

Morgan Stanley (MS) delivered a beat on both its top and bottom line in its third quarter, as banks continued to shine.

U.S. import and export prices fell more than expected in September.

data.title: Maxeon CEO Bill Mulligan to retire early next year
data.description:
• Maxeon Solar Technologies (NASDAQ:MAXN) on Wednesday announced a leadership transition, with CEO Bill Mulligan set to retire at the end of January 2025.
  • George Guo, formerly an advisor to the chairman of TCL Group and previous CEO of TCL Communication Technology, assumed the role of Maxeon CEO and joined its board of directors effective October 11, 2024.
  • Mulligan will aid the leadership transition through January.

data.title: S&W Seed Company provides update on delay of Form 10-K filing for FY 2024
data.description:
• S&W Seed Company (NASDAQ:SANW) on Wednesday, provided an update regarding the late filing of its annual report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2024.
  • The company said it is continuing to work through reporting requirements as a component of the previously announced voluntary plan of administration for its subsidiary, S&W Seed Company Australia Pty Ltd.
  • Due to the change in its reportable segments, the company is analyzing the impacts of change under ASC 250, Accounting Changes and Error Corrections, and is working to prepare all necessary segment disclosures with a goal to filing its 10-K as soon as possible.

data.title: Wave stock soars 38% on positive data for AATD therapy WVE-006
data.description:
Wave Life Sciences (NASDAQ:WVE) shares soared 38% in premarket trading Wednesday after the company announced positive proof-of-mechanism data from its Phase 1b/2a RestoraAATion-2 study for its therapy candidate WVE-006 in the treatment of alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency, or AATD.

The therapy was developed using Wave’s oligonucleotide chemistry platform and is designed to address AATD-related lung disease and/or liver disease. Wave said the proof-of-mechanism data are “the first-ever clinical demonstration of RNA editing in humans,” according to a statement.

The company expects to release multi-dose data from the study next year.

U.K. drugmaker GSK (GSK) holds the exclusive global license for WVE-006 and will assume development and commercialization responsibilities for the product after Wave wraps up the RestorAATion-2 study. Wave said it is eligible to receive up to $525M in milestone payments plus tiered royalties on net sales for WVE-006 under the GSK agreement.

Wave added that it plans to highlight the WVE-006 data along with data from its GalNAc-RNA editing programs and preclinical data for its obesity drug candidate WVE-007 during its Annual Research Day on Oct. 30.

data.title: Eastman Chemical, CF Industries downgraded at Bank of America
data.description:
Eastman Chemical (NYSE:EMN) and CF Industries Holdings (NYSE:CF) on Wednesday were downgraded by analysts at Bank of America.

Eastman Chemical downgrade

Eastman’s (EMN) investment rating was lowered to Neutral from Buy previously. After rallying by about 57% in the past 12 months, Eastman’s (EMN) “current valuation leaves little room for upside potential,” according to Bank of America.

“This upside hinges in our view on the success of the company’s methanolysis strategy (polyester renewal technology, or PRT), which carries elevated risk,” Salvator Tiano, analyst at Bank of America, said in an October 16 report.

The bank raised its price target on Eastman (EMN) to $119 a share from $114 a share previously by rolling over its forecast to 2025 from 2024, and cutting the enterprise value-to-earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization multiple to 8.5 times from 9.5 times, and the price-to-earnings multiple to 11.5 times from 13 times.

CF Industries downgrade

CF Industries’ (CF) rating was lowered to Underperform from Neutral previously. Bank of America foresees weaker pricing for fertilizer.

“We believe the rally in global nitrogen prices is likely to lose momentum in the coming months, ultimately pushing earnings lower in 2025,” Steven Byrne, analyst at Bank of America, said.

The bank maintained its price target for CF Industries (CF) at $83 a share, based on an estimated enterprise value that’s 8 times earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization.

Upgrades of Westlake, Corteva

Bank of America upgraded Westlake (WLK) to Neutral from Underperform, and Corteva (CTVA) to Buy from Neutral previously.

data.title: Amazon latest tech giant to sign deals to invest in small nuclear reactors to power AI
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) said Wednesday it is investing in small nuclear reactors, coming just two days after a similar announcement by Google; advanced nuclear tech companies NuScale Power (NYSE:SMR) +8.3%, Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) +8.1% and Nano Nuclear Energy (NNE) +7.8% pre-market.

Amazon Web Services (AMZN) said it signed an agreement with Dominion Energy (D) to explore the development of a small module nuclear reactor near Dominion's existing North Anna nuclear power station.

Amazon (AMZN) also will invest in reactor developer X-energy for its early development work, and collaborate with regional utility consortium Energy Northwest in Washington state to support construction of four X-energy reactors there.

data.title: GoodRx to offer pay-over-time option with Affirm
data.description:
GoodRx (NASDAQ:GDRX) is partnering with retail pharmacies to offer pay-over-time option directly to consumers.

Approved customers will now be able to select an Affirm (NASDAQ:AFRM) payment plan for select medications directly on the GoodRx (GDRX) platform, the telemedicine platform said.

GoodRx is launching this program with its partner pharmacies, including Hy-Vee, a grocery retailer with a network of more than 275 retail pharmacies. This program will start rolling out in the coming months.

“Through this new technology solution, we’re offering a unique capability that will help people access their medications - whether it’s helping them reach a deductible or providing great financial flexibility and control,” said Scott Wagner, interim CEO at GoodRx.

data.title: Airbnb expands options for property owners to find co-hosts
data.description:
Airbnb (NASDAQ:ABNB) introduced a new program on Wednesday that will allow property owners to easily find and hire the best local co-hosts to manage their Airbnb (ABNB) listings. Co-hosts are being set up to provide personalized support based on hosting needs, from listing setup to managing bookings, and communicating with guests.

Airbnb (ABNB) said it has more than 10,000 co-hosts across 10 countries. The co-hosts were highlighted as being experienced hosts with an exceptional track record on Airbnb (ABNB). They have an average rating1 of 4.86, compared to 4.62 for large property management companies.

"With the Co-Host Network, we’re taking the work out of hosting," stated Airbnb (ABNB) CEO said Brian Chesky. "Starting today, you provide the home, and we’ll provide an exceptional co-host," he added.

Airbnb (ABNB) also said that it made more than 50 upgrades for guests, including a more personalized app from search to booking.

Shares of Airbnb (ABNB) were up 0.7% in premarket trading as they continue to slowly recover from the recent low when Q2 earnings were released. On a year-to-date basis, Airbnb (ABNB) is down 2.1%.

data.title: ACNB declares $0.32 dividend
data.description:
• ACNB (NASDAQ:ACNB) declared $0.32/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.0%
  • Payable Dec. 13; for shareholders of record Nov. 29; ex-div Nov. 29.
  • See ACNB Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
